subject: Biology
question: Which cell organelle is primarily responsible for generating the chemical energy carrier adenosine triphosphate (ATP)?

poll_explanation.why: Mitochondria are the double-membrane organelle systems that function as the main site of aerobic respiration and cellular ATP production.
poll_explanation.poll_hint: Mitochondria generate ATP (adenosine triphosphate) through cellular aerobic respiration, functioning as the cellular power generator.

options_analysis.why: Incorrect. The nucleus serves as the repository of genetic instructions and cell control.
Incorrect. Ribosomes serve as the physical site of protein translation.
Correct. Mitochondria perform cellular respiration to generate ATP energy.
Incorrect. Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes to break down waste.
